cover.headline_html: 3 myths quietly holding new GovCon shops back.

myths.label: The Past-Performance Trap
myths.myth_text: You need years of federal contract history before you can even apply for a GSA Schedule.
myths.reality_text: The Schedule itself is how you build that history. You don't need federal past performance to apply — you need commercial revenue and two years in business.

myths.label: The Big-Company Illusion
myths.myth_text: Federal contracting is dominated by large primes. Small businesses can't compete.
myths.reality_text: 23% of all federal contract spending is legally mandated for small businesses. Agencies have annual goals they must hit — and they actively need small business vendors to fill them.

myths.label: The Price Race
myths.myth_text: You need to undercut everyone on price to win federal bids.
myths.reality_text: Most federal solicitations use best-value evaluation — quality, past performance, and capability all count. Chronic undercutting kills your margins and signals risk to contracting officers.

close.close_question: Drop the myth you believed when you first started looking at federal contracting. What finally changed your mind?

template_data.image_headline: THREE MYTHS HOLDING YOUR GOVCON BACK
template_data.image_prompt: Carousel cover infographic. Dark navy background. Bold white headline top: 'THREE MYTHS HOLDING YOU BACK'. Three numbered red callout tiles stacked vertically, each with a 2-3 word short myth label: '01 NO PAST PERFORMANCE', '02 TOO SMALL TO WIN', '03 PRICE RACE'. Right side: illustrated professional woman in blazer looking confident, arms crossed. Bottom strip in green: bold label 'THE REALITY SWIPE'. Footer text: 'Josh Ladick · GSA Focus'.
